3.description: 211 Illinois is a free statewide information and referral service connecting Illinois residents to health and human services including housing, food, utilities, employment, and crisis support. United Way-supported navigators help callers find local programs by need and ZIP code. 211 Illinois is a referral line—not a direct-service provider. This resource supports Illinoisans navigating reentry in throughout Illinois.

4.description: IDHS — Benefits & Family Services Overview is a statewide or regional referral resource for Illinoisans affected by the criminal justice system. Illinois Department of Human Services administers SNAP, Medicaid, TANF, and child care assistance through Family Community Resource Centers in every county and the ABE online portal. IDHS Pre-Release Program accepts SNAP and medical applications from approved IDOC facilities within ten days of release. Users can search by location, service type, or need to find active providers in throughout Illinois. Specialists or online tools help match people to housing, treatment, employment, legal aid, or benefits resources operated by other organizations. This entry describes a navigation hub—staff here do not replace direct services at partner agencies.

10.description: New Leaf Illinois — Record Relief Network serves justice-involved adults and families rebuilding stability after incarceration in throughout Illinois. New Leaf Illinois is a statewide network of legal aid organizations helping eligible Illinois residents seal or clear criminal records under state law. Navigators connect callers to free legal representation through partner organizations for expungement and sealing—not active criminal defense cases. Representation is limited to civil legal matters—not active criminal cases. Income, household size, and offense type determine eligibility for record relief. Contact this resource to confirm current intake steps, required documents, and whether walk-ins or referrals are accepted.
10.description_es: New Leaf Illinois — Record Relief Network ofrece asistencia legal civil gratuita o de bajo costo para residentes de bajos ingresos en en todo Illinois. Los abogados ayudan con sellado o eliminación de antecedentes, desalojos, beneficios y otros asuntos civiles—no casos penales activos. La representación se limita a asuntos legales civiles—no casos penales activos. Ingresos, tamaño del hogar y tipo de delito determinan la elegibilidad para alivio de antecedentes. Contacte la oficina para confirmar pasos de admisión, documentos requeridos y si aceptan visitas sin cita o referencias.

11.description: IL-AFLAN — Illinois Armed Forces Legal Aid Network serves justice-involved adults and families rebuilding stability after incarceration in throughout Illinois. Hotline connects callers to pro bono attorneys—not VA medical emergency services. Illinois HFS administers Medicaid, All Kids, and managed care programs statewide helping returning citizens verify enrollment and connect to health plans after ABE application. Criminal record is generally not a barrier to Medicaid eligibility for income-qualified Illinois residents. Services are available on a sliding fee or Medicaid basis for eligible patients; bring ID and release paperwork when establishing care after incarceration. 19.description: Illinois Criminal Justice Information Authority serves justice-involved adults and families rebuilding stability after incarceration in throughout Illinois.
